summary: A small item-system matching PR applying wins found by a new permuter. It touched Link boomerang, Link hookshot, and Ness yoyo item code, mostly by adding tiny helper inlines, changing local declaration/order patterns, inserting temporary variables, and adjusting stack padding to improve or recover matches. The PR body states the motivation directly: "New permuter yielded a few wins" and reports progress as "Remaining item TUs: 21" and "Remaining item functions: 69".
what_changed: In itlinkboomerang.c, it_802A0534 now assigns gobj->hsd_obj through an intermediate HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp before assigning jobj, a local-shaping change intended for matching.
In itlinkhookshot.c, fn_802A33A0 gained a small static inline helper fn_802A33A0_GetFighter(Item* item) returning item->owner->user_data, and the local declaration order was changed.

In fn_802A33A0, the it_802A5E28 call changed its final argument from fp->x40 to attr->x40. The diff does not explain whether this is a semantic bug fix, a discovered field equivalence, or a corrected decompilation; it is the most behavior-relevant-looking change in the slice.

In it_802A39FC, locals were initialized closer to declaration, an int var_r0 collision flag became bool chk, stack padding was removed from the shown region, and a nested y_delta temporary was introduced before assigning temp_f30.
In it_802A39FC, the two mpCheckAllRemap checks now set chk true/false and the branch tests if (chk), preserving the apparent control-flow intent while changing local typing and codegen shape.
In it_802A4758, a static inline it_802A4758_permuterslop(ItemLink* link_0) was added to return &link_0->pos, and test_comp now receives that helper result instead of &link_0->pos directly.
In itnessyoyo.c, rotation update logic from itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim was split into static inline itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation(Item* ip).
In itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim, the function now gets Item* ip, keeps PAD_STACK(16), calls the new UpdateRotation inline, then continues with itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_inline and it_802BE958_inline.
smart_moves: Used very small static inline helpers to influence instruction selection/register allocation without changing the apparent high-level algorithm.

Preserved suspicious matching-only changes with explicit comments such as "// permuterslop", making it easier for future maintainers to distinguish decompilation shaping from intentional domain abstraction.
Isolated a yoyo rotation block into an inline helper, a common tactic for matching when the compiler wants a separate lifetime/stack shape while still inlining into the caller.
Replaced an integer flag with bool chk in the hookshot collision path, making the source closer to the binary's likely boolean branch pattern while preserving readability.
Introduced intermediate temporaries like jobj_tmp and y_delta only where needed, keeping the patches narrow and targeted to permuter-discovered wins.
decomp_lessons: Permuter output can reveal that trivial-looking source rewrites, such as assigning through an extra local or passing a field address through an inline helper, are enough to produce the desired codegen.
For item code, local declaration order and initialization placement can matter for stack/register layout; moving GET_ITEM-derived initializations to declarations may improve matching.
Tiny inline accessors are useful when direct field/member expressions produce the wrong code shape, especially around owner/user_data retrieval and struct member address-taking.
When a source expression semantically names the same kind of data but emits differently, try wrapping it in a static inline rather than larger control-flow rewrites.
Stack padding values may need to be adjusted after extracting inline helpers; this PR changed Ness yoyo padding from a larger single-function shape to PAD_STACK(16) in both helper/caller context.
Keep permuter-motivated artifacts localized and named clearly so they can be revisited after surrounding functions or types improve.
naming_conventions: New helper names were tied to the parent function, e.g. fn_802A33A0_GetFighter and it_802A4758_permuterslop.
The PR uses explicit matching-workaround terminology in names/comments such as it_802A4758_permuterslop and "// permuterslop".
Extracted yoyo helper used a descriptive suffix: itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation.
Temporary locals were named mechanically after decompiler/permuter needs, such as jobj_tmp, temp_f30_2, y_delta, and chk.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add an intermediate pointer local before using an object field: gobj->hsd_obj -> jobj_tmp -> jobj.
Create static inline wrappers around field access or address-taking to alter emitted code while keeping the operation inline.
Reorder local declarations and move initialization to declarations to affect variable lifetimes and register allocation.
Use a bool flag for conditional collision checks instead of an int when the emitted branch pattern benefits.
Introduce nested-scope temporaries, such as y_delta, to influence floating-point register lifetimes.
Split a chunk of logic into a static inline helper and call it from the original function to reshape stack padding and local lifetimes.
Adjust PAD_STACK after helper extraction; the Ness yoyo diff shows PAD_STACK(24) being replaced by PAD_STACK(16) in the extracted rotation helper path and PAD_STACK(16) in the caller.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body comment from the author says: "New permuter yielded a few wins. :)" and reports remaining item work counts.
There is no evidence in the provided slice of reviewer-requested naming, struct, or semantic changes.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for removal or renaming of helpers marked permuterslop.
Check whether attr->x40 in fn_802A33A0 was documented or validated in later hookshot attribute struct work.
Look for subsequent item TU progress reports to see which of the remaining 21 item TUs or 69 functions were closed next.
Search for similar static inline accessor tactics around owner->user_data and member address-taking in other item files.
